// 001 — Experience

Where I've
worked & competed.

Three years building race cars, three summers in aerospace manufacturing, plus rocketry and efficiency racing.

Revolve NTNU — Formula Student
2022–2025
Chassis Structural (Year 3) · Monocoque Design (Year 2) · Aero Design (Year 1)

Year 3: Responsible for full structural dimensioning of the CFRP monocoque. Analytical and numerical methods including FEA in Abaqus. Destructive testing of sandwich panels (punch shear, 3-point bending) to generate material data for the Structural Equivalency Sheet.

Year 2: Complete monocoque ownership — packaging, design, manufacturing design of a CFRP / aluminium honeycomb sandwich panel chassis.

Year 1: Aerodynamic and manufacturing design of a CFRP foam-core front wing.

GKN Aerospace
3 summers
Engineering Intern — Machining & Engine Components

Three consecutive summer placements working on precision metal machining of aero engine components. Exposure to aerospace quality systems, engineering tolerancing, and the gap between design intent and what is actually manufacturable. Direct experience with the standards and processes that underpin certifiable aerospace structures.

Aerospace standards Precision machining Engine components Manufacturing tolerancing AS9100 environment
Student Rocketry
1 year
Structures Engineer

Structural design and analysis of CFRP airframe components for a student rocket. Load analysis for launch, flight, and recovery phases. Buckling and strength checks on thin-walled composite tubes.

CFRP airframe Launch load analysis Buckling checks Thin-walled structures
Shell Eco-Marathon
½ year
Composites / Bodywork

Ultra-lightweight CFRP composite bodywork for a Shell Eco-Marathon efficiency vehicle. Female mould manufacture, hand layup, and structural sizing for an enclosure where every gram is a loss.

Lightweight structures Hand layup Female mould Weight optimisation
// 002 — Projects

Selected
work.

Structural and composites projects across competition, academia, and industry.

02
FS Monocoque — Full Design & Manufacturing

Complete ownership of the Year 2 CFRP / alu honeycomb sandwich monocoque. Packaging from scratch, structural design, layup schedule, and manufacturing process. Demoulded and competition-ready.

CFRP designSandwich panelCATIAManufacturing
03
Monocoque Structural Dimensioning & SES

Year 3 — responsible for proving the monocoque meets FSG structural equivalency requirements. Analytical calculations, Abaqus FEA, and physical destructive testing of sandwich coupons (punch shear and 3-point bending) to characterise stiffness and strength.

Abaqus FEASESDestructive testingPython
01
Front Wing — Aero & Structural Design

Year 1 — aerodynamic profile selection and CFRP foam-core sandwich wing design. Structural sizing for torsional stiffness and tip-load cases. Manufacturing design for female-mould wet layup.

Foam core sandwichCFRP wingAero designSolidWorks
04
MSc Thesis — Composites FEA (ongoing)

Master's thesis on finite element analysis of composite structures. Details to follow on completion spring 2026. Focus on accurate FEA modelling methodology for laminated composite components.

// 005 — About

The person
behind it.

I'm an MSc composites engineering candidate at NTNU, graduating spring 2026. My work focuses on structural design and FEA of composite structures — specifically the kind of highly loaded, weight-critical components where getting the analysis right actually matters.

Three years with Revolve NTNU took me from manufacturing a foam-core front wing in Year 1, to owning the full monocoque design in Year 2, to being responsible for structural dimensioning and writing the Structural Equivalency Sheet in Year 3. That progression — from making things to proving they're safe — is the core of what I do.

Three summers at GKN Aerospace taught me what engineering looks like when certification is the end goal, not just competition rules. That context shapes how I think about design: manufacturable, verifiable, and defensible.

I'm drawn to roles in F1 and high-performance motorsport or aerospace structures — places where composite design decisions have real consequences and the margin for error is close to zero.

StatusMSc candidate — graduating spring 2026
